meaning: n.鬼,幽灵vt.鬼怪般地出没于,惊吓vi.逃窜
examples: That might spook the buyers of bonds that finance all those deficits.
Televiewers, sick of hoary Hoot Gibson oaters and antique spook comedies, wonder when, if ever, they will see fresh, first-class Hollywood films.
As for what to do with a haunted house, the methods vary; running out the front door screaming is one option, but you can also get to know the spook.
Both are immersed in corruption scandals, and concerns about probity will spook financial markets.
EU. Even so, big deficits plus any new wave of financial panic could yet spook investors.
